what_it_does: This file is the module constitution for the file-discovered mode-detection-rule registry. It defines the schema, discovery guarantees, priority ordering, seed rules, and usage contract for self-registering detection rules that map an activity-signal snapshot to a candidate presence mode.
observations: It declares itself a constitution with prescriptive register and living status.
A mode-detection-rule is a row mapping a SIGNAL CONDITION to a candidate presence mode.
The detector walks discovered rules in priority order, first-match-wins, and feeds the existing Suite.autodetect_mode toggle.
Rules are file-discovered: one self-registering mode_detection_rules/<id>.py per rule, mirroring roles/projections/node-types.
Rule conditions must be runtime/rules.py RULE_OPS data-ASTs, validated at discovery and evaluated by rules.evaluate; lambdas/eval/strings are forbidden.
Required schema per rule: id, candidate, why, when, priority; id must equal the file stem; candidate is validated at detect time against suite.MODES.
Ordering is by declared priority integer then id tiebreak, never by filename/listdir.
It prescribes a None-handling discipline: idle_seconds may be None, so idle-threshold rules must guard with {and: [ {ne: [idle_seconds, lit None]}, ... ]}.
It lists three seed rules: background (priority 10), focus (priority 20), listening (priority 30).
New rules are added by dropping a file and updating this drift-home document; tests/mode_autodetect_acceptance.py asserts reflection.
The registry is instantiated in Suite.__init__ and the detector reads ordered() plus activity_signal(); propose_mode feeds autodetect_mode.
It explicitly forbids the detector from calling set_mode directly or performing any consequential action.
standouts: Replaces a hardcoded lambda-based Python list with a file-discovered data-AST registry (registry-is-truth).
Ordering is load-bearing first-match-wins by declared priority, unlike unordered role/projection registries.
Explicit defensive None-handling for idle_seconds inside the data-AST.
The detector is only a candidate producer; the toggle owns posture changes.
conventions: Follows the Obsidian-vault constitution format with YAML frontmatter, aliases, tags, relates-to, and living status.
Follows the self-registering file-drop registry pattern used by roles, projections, and node-types.
Uses fail-loud discovery validation for malformed entries.
Reuses the G3 rule-engine RULE_OPS data-AST as the single predicate language.
Requires the drift-home document to stay synchronized with the test acceptance suite.
concerns: Candidate membership in suite.MODES is deferred to detect time, so invalid candidates are not caught at discovery.
Manual sync burden between discovered files and this drift-home document enforced by a test.
None-handling relies on authors manually writing guard clauses rather than schema-level nullable safety.
Priority ordering uses plain integers with no documented range or collision policy beyond id tiebreak.
notes: This is the constitution that turns mode-detection rules into file-droppable, validated data-AST registry entries feeding an existing autodetect toggle.
questions: What is the full shape and typing of the activity_signal() snapshot?
How does ModeDetectionRuleRegistry differ concretely from projection/role registries beyond ordering?
What is the exact content and verification mechanism of tests/mode_autodetect_acceptance.py?
What are the full grammar and evaluation semantics of rules.RULE_OPS?
Where is suite.MODES defined and what is the complete set of valid modes?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file both declares the registry constitution and documents the live seed rules, seam, and operational contract.
apparent_themes: Reuse-don't-parallel (one predicate language, one registry mechanism).
Separation of detection (candidate production) from posture toggle (decision/effect).
Explicit order-bearing semantics decoupled from filenames.
Defensive handling of missing/None startup state.
intention_signals: It is trying to eliminate hardcoded mode-detection logic and lambdas by promoting rules to first-class, self-registering, data-driven registry entries that can be authored as files, while preserving deterministic priority-ordered first-match detection and keeping the detector a pure candidate producer separate from mode switching.
novelty: The explicit rejection of lambda/eval conditions in favor of a shared G3 data-AST predicate language for a simple detector, and the insistence that adding a rule means dropping a file rather than editing code, with ordering by declared priority rather than filename.
contribution: A clear, validated, file-discovered schema and contract for mode-detection rules; the seed rule set and priority ordering; the None-handling pattern; and the boundary that the detector produces candidates but does not switch modes.
summary_for_embedding: This file is the living constitution for a file-discovered, priority-ordered registry of mode-detection rules that use a shared data-AST predicate language to map activity-signal snapshots into candidate presence modes for an autodetect toggle.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to replace hardcoded lambda-based detection rules with self-registering, authorable data files that are validated at discovery and ordered by explicit priority.
